mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with falling FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of observed cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when the economy is in a recovery phase.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% monthly, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces positive forward returns in < 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth rates directly increase corporate revenue expectations and equity cash flow projections, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the industrial production index below its 6-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its 6-month average, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.
